response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries which became the foundation for their muffin making adventure. Her collection was the starting point for the group's efforts and inspired David to collect more. David collected 15 more blueberries than Emily which meant he had 39 blueberries in total. His collection was not only larger but also three times as many as what Sophia had gathered.

***

David's collection was crucial for the overall number of blueberries they had available. Sophia's contribution although smaller was still significant. When you divide David's total by three you find that Sophia had 13 blueberries. Adding all three together gives a total of 76 blueberries which they used for making muffins. Each batch required 9 blueberries which meant they could make several batches.

***

They divided the blueberries into batches of 9 and calculated how many they could make. With 76 blueberries they were able to make 8 full batches with 4 blueberries left over. Each batch was sold for 5 dollars which meant they earned money based on the number of batches they could produce. The leftover blueberries were not enough to make another batch so they were not used for selling.

***

The total money they made from the blueberries was 40 dollars. This amount came from selling 8 batches of muffins at 5 dollars each. The process of collecting and selling the blueberries was a rewarding experience for all involved. It brought them together and highlighted the value of teamwork and youth.
